rationale: In the theory development, the citing paper uses Gershon et al. (2020) as evidence that reward attainment can create positive affect and potentially increase subsequent engagement, contrasting pure postreward-resetting logic. The citation is revisited in the results/discussion to interpret why attained rewards can raise game engagement in their data. While the citing paper does not build on the referral-specific context of Gershon et al., it uses that study as meaningful conceptual support for a key reward-attainment mechanism in its framing and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Gershon et al. (2020) to support the idea that attaining rewards can produce positive affect and motivate behavior. This prior evidence helps ground the citing paper's conceptual setup and interpretation of its reward-attainment effects.
